# Phase Transitions

## Explains

Growth creates qualitative discontinuities where the nature of coordination must
fundamentally change.

## The Concept

Solo → Startup → Scaleup → Enterprise aren't points on a smooth curve. They're
different KINDS of organization that require different coordination mechanisms.
What works at one scale doesn't just work less well at the next — it actively
breaks. A startup's all-hands meeting becomes impossible at 200 people. An
enterprise's committee structure paralyzes a 5-person team.

These are phase transitions — the system must reorganize, not just scale. Like
water becoming ice or steam, the underlying elements are the same but their
arrangement and interactions change fundamentally. You cannot get from liquid to
gas by being "more liquid." You must cross a boundary where the rules change.

The framework handles this through configurable domains and scale profiles, but
the deeper insight is: expect your governance to break at boundaries, and design
for reorganization, not just growth. A governance system that works perfectly at
one scale and fails catastrophically at the next is not robust — it's brittle in
a way that only becomes visible at the worst possible moment.

The practical implication: governance must be designed with explicit transition
points. Not "this will scale smoothly" but "this works until N, then we switch
to that." Acknowledging discontinuity upfront prevents the crisis of discovering
it under pressure.

## Why This Matters for Governance

JOS governs ventures across wildly different scales. A governance framework that
assumes smooth scaling will impose enterprise overhead on solo operators or leave
enterprises with startup-grade controls. Neither is acceptable.

Phase transitions explain why JOS needs scale profiles, domain configurations,
and explicit boundary conditions. The same principles apply everywhere, but their
implementation must reorganize at phase boundaries. Fractal self-similarity
provides the pattern; phase transitions explain where the pattern must be
re-instantiated at a new level.

## Connects To

- JOS-C10 (Emergent Complexity)
- JOS-C01 (Fractal Self-Similarity)
- Framework §5 (domain scale profiles)

## Provenance

GOLD #12 (Phase Transitions as Qualitative Shifts), panel observation about
holonic self-similarity limits.

### Framework Connection

Framework §5 (scale-adaptive domain profiles) and §8 (slot dynamics with birth/kill) provide the governance machinery for phase transitions — domains reconfigure at scale boundaries, and slots are born or killed as the system reorganizes across phases.
